content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create the final report:

---

## COMPLETE RUNTIME ENVIRONMENT ANALYSIS

### 1. ENTRY POINT & ROOT SETUP

**Entry Point:** `/src/index.jsx`
- Creates React root in `#root` element (from `index.html`)
- Imports global CSS: `/src/core-ui/index.css`
- Renders `<App />` component

**HTML Root:** `/index.html`
- Root element: `<main id="root">`
- Preloads Google reCAPTCHA API
- Links core-ui CSS at `/src/core-ui/index.css`

**Core UI Stylesheet:** `/src/core-ui/index.css`
- Imports Tailwind CSS (`@import "tailwindcss"`)
- CSS Variables:
  - `--primary-font-color: rgb(147, 157, 250)` (indigo)
  - `--primary-font-color-hover: rgb(82, 98, 245)` (darker indigo)
- System font stack with `-webkit-font-smoothing` antialiasing

---

### 2. APP.TSX & ROOT PROVIDER STRUCTURE

**Main Component:** `/src/App.jsx`

**Providers/Context:**
- React Router (`BrowserRouter`) - wraps entire app
- No explicit context providers, state managed at App level
- Uses hooks: `useState`, `useEffect`

**Global State Management (in App.jsx):**
```javascript
- user: null | {id, createdTime, email, fullname, gender, plan, password}
- loggedIn: boolean
- triggeredLogout: boolean
```

**Session Storage Usage:**
- `sessionStorage.getItem/setItem("loggedIn")` - track login status
- `sessionStorage.getItem/setItem("user")` - store user JSON object
- Clears on logout via `sessionStorage.removeItem()`

---

### 3. ROUTES & PAGES

**Router Structure:** React Router v7.7.0 with dynamic conditional rendering based on `loggedIn` state

| Route | Component | Auth Required |
|-------|-----------|---|
| `/` | `Landing` | No |
| `/contact` | `Contact` | No |
| `/pricing` | `Pricing` | No |
| `/faq` | `Faq` | No |
| `/blog` | `Blog` | No |
| `/blog/:name` | `BlogPost` | No |
| `/about` | `About` | No |
| `/terms` | `Terms` | No |
| `/refunds` | `Refunds` | No |
| `/privacy` | `Privacy` | No |
| `/jobs` | `Jobs` | No |
| `/sign-in` | `SignIn` \| `Profile` | Conditional |
| `/sign-up` | `SignUp` \| `Profile` | Conditional |
| `/profile` | `Profile` | Yes (403 -> NotFound) |
| `/*` | `NotFound` | No |

**Page Components Location:** `/src/routes/`
- `landing/` - Landing page with Hero, Partners, Features, CTA, Stats, Pricing, Testimonials, Team, Comparison, Newsletter
- `sign-in/` - SignIn.jsx + SignInForm.jsx
- `sign-up/` - SignUp.jsx + SignUpForm.jsx
- `user-profile/` - Profile.jsx (user dashboard)
- `contact/` - Contact.jsx + ContactForm.jsx + CareerCta.jsx + ContactImg.jsx
- `pricing/` - Pricing.jsx + PricingComparisonTable.jsx + PricingFaq.jsx
- `blog/` - Blog.jsx + BlogGrid.jsx
- `blog-post/` - BlogPost.jsx
- `about/` - About.jsx + AboutOne.jsx + AboutTwo.jsx + AboutThree.jsx
- `faq/` - Faq.jsx + FaqList.jsx
- `jobs/` - Jobs.jsx + JobsListing.jsx
- `privacy/`, `refunds/`, `terms/` - Static content pages

---

### 4. REUSABLE UI COMPONENTS

**Shared Components:** `/src/components/`

| Component | File | Props | Key Features |
|-----------|------|-------|---|
| `Navigation` | `navigation/Navigation.jsx` | `loggedIn`, `setLoggedIn`, `setTriggeredLogout` | Header nav with auth-aware buttons, logo, link menu |
| `Footer` | `footer/Footer.jsx` | None | Multi-column footer (Solutions, Support, Company, Legal), social icons |
| `Cta` | `cta/Cta.jsx` | `loggedIn` | "Ready to dive in?" CTA section with conditional link |
| `Newsletter` | `Newsletter.jsx` | None | Email signup form with validation, success/error states |
| `Team` | `team/Team.jsx` | None | Team member grid from static data |
| `NotFound` | `NotFound.jsx` | None | 404 page |
| `JobsForm` | `JobsForm.jsx` | None | Job application form with validation |

**Example Components (Storybook):** `/src/stories/`
- `Button.jsx` - Example button component with primary/secondary variants
- `Header.jsx` - Example header component
- `Page.jsx` - Example page component
- CSS: `button.css`, `header.css`, `page.css` (Storybook example styling)

---

### 5. FETCH/API CALLS & ENDPOINTS

**Airtable Integration:**
- Module: `/src/airtable/airtable.js`
- Base instance initialized with `VITE_AIRTABLE_KEY` and `VITE_AIRTABLE_ID`
- Table: `"users"` - manages user records

**API URLs (from environment variables):**

| Function | Method | URL Env Var | Purpose |
|----------|--------|-----------|---------|
| `retrieveDatabase()` | GET | `VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL` | Fetch all users from Airtable |
| `updateUser()` | POST | `VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL` | Update user field |
| `registerUser()` | CREATE via Airtable SDK | - | Create new user in Airtable |
| `deleteUser()` | DESTROY via Airtable SDK | - | Delete user from Airtable |
| `verifyCaptcha()` | POST | `VITE_SERVER_URL` | Verify reCAPTCHA token |

**Backend Server:** `/api/index.mjs`
- Express.js server on port 3000 (default)
- Endpoints:
  - `GET /` - health check
  - `GET /airtable` - fetch users from Airtable
  - `POST /airtable` - update user data
  - `POST /verify-recaptcha` - verify Google reCAPTCHA tokens
- CORS enabled for all origins
- Environment variables: `AIRTABLE_KEY`, `AIRTABLE_ID`, `CAPTCHA_SECRET`

---

### 6. BROWSER STATE & STORAGE

**sessionStorage Keys:**
- `"loggedIn"` - string "true" when user authenticated
- `"user"` - JSON stringified user object:
  ```javascript
  {
    id: string (UUID),
    createdTime: string (YYYY-MM-DD),
    email: string,
    fullname: string,
    gender: string,
    plan: string,
    password: string
  }
  ```

**Browser APIs Used:**
- `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` - scroll to top (called on navigation)
- `sessionStorage` - session-based auth persistence
- `import.meta.env.*` - Vite environment variables

**No usage of:**
- localStorage (session-only)
- Cookies (none set)
- IndexedDB

---

### 7. MODAL/PORTAL USAGE

**Modal Implementation:** CSS-based fixed overlays (no createPortal)

Modal Locations:
1. **Profile.jsx** - Delete account confirmation dialog
   - Condition: `{deleteRequest && (<div className="fixed inset-0 z-10...">)}`
   - Backdrop: `bg-gray-900/80` (semi-transparent overlay)
   - Buttons: Delete / Cancel

2. **Profile.jsx** - Change password modal
   - Condition: `{editPassword && (<div className="fixed inset-0 z-10...">)}`
   - Form with password inputs
   - Buttons: Change password / Cancel

**No React portals used** - all modals rendered in DOM at component location with fixed positioning

---

### 8. DEPENDENCIES & ENVIRONMENT

**package.json Details:**

**Core Dependencies:**
- `react` 18.3.1
- `react-dom` 18.3.1
- `react-router-dom` 7.7.0
- `@headlessui/react` 1.5.0
- `@tailwindcss/vite` 4.1.11
- `react-google-recaptcha` 3.1.0

**Backend Dependencies:**
- `express` 4.18.2
- `cors` 2.8.5
- `airtable` 0.12.2
- `dotenv` 17.2.0
- `node-fetch` 3.3.2

**Dev Dependencies (Storybook & Testing):**
- `storybook` 0.0.0-pr-34421-sha-8babe7bc (PR version)
- `@storybook/react-vite` (same PR version)
- `@storybook/addon-vitest`
- `@storybook/addon-a11y`
- `@storybook/addon-docs`
- `@storybook/addon-onboarding`
- `@chromatic-com/storybook` 5.1.1
- `vitest` 4.1.2
- `playwright` 1.58.2
- `vite` 7.0.5

**Utility Libraries:**
- `moment` 2.30.1 (date formatting)
- `uuid` 11.1.0 (ID generation)
- `prop-types` 15.8.1

---

### 9. STORYBOOK CONFIGURATION

**Config File:** `/storybook/main.ts`
```typescript
stories: [
  '../src/**/*.mdx',
  '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)',
  './eval-support/*.mdx',
]
addons: [
  '@chromatic-com/storybook',
  '@storybook/addon-vitest',
  '@storybook/addon-a11y',
  '@storybook/addon-docs',
  '@storybook/addon-onboarding',
]
framework: '@storybook/react-vite'
```

**Preview Config:** `/storybook/preview.tsx`
- Control matchers for color and date attributes
- A11y addon with "todo" test setting

**Existing Stories:** `/src/stories/`
- `Button.stories.js` - 4 variants (Primary, Secondary, Large, Small)
- `Header.stories.js` - 2 states (LoggedIn, LoggedOut)
- `Page.stories.js` - Example page story

**Vite Config Integration:** `/vite.config.js`
- Storybook vitest plugin configured
- Browser testing with Playwright enabled
- Responsive testing for mobile (iPhone 17 spec)

---

### 10. STYLING & THEMING

**Tailwind CSS:**
- Config: `/tailwind.config.js`
- Content paths: `./index.html` and `./src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}`
- Using Tailwind v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite`

**Color Scheme:**
- Background: `bg-gray-900` (dark theme)
- Primary accent: `bg-indigo-500/600/700` (various states)
- Text: `text-white`, `text-gray-300`, `text-gray-400`, `text-gray-600`
- CSS Variables override: `color:var(--primary-font-color)`

**No custom CSS files** (besides Storybook example styles)

---

### 11. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
/src
├── index.jsx                    # Entry point
├── App.jsx                      # Root component with routing
├── core-ui/
│   └── index.css               # Global Tailwind + CSS vars
├── airtable/
│   └── airtable.js             # Airtable base instance
├── components/                  # Shared components
│   ├── navigation/
│   │   └── Navigation.jsx
│   ├── footer/
│   │   └── Footer.jsx
│   ├── cta/
│   │   └── Cta.jsx
│   ├── team/
│   │   └── Team.jsx
│   ├── Newsletter.jsx
│   ├── JobsForm.jsx
│   └── NotFound.jsx
├── routes/                      # Page components
│   ├── landing/
│   ├── sign-in/
│   ├── sign-up/
│   ├── user-profile/
│   ├── contact/
│   ├── pricing/
│   ├── blog/
│   ├── blog-post/
│   ├── about/
│   ├── faq/
│   ├── jobs/
│   ├── privacy/
│   ├── refunds/
│   └── terms/
├── data/                        # Static data files
│   ├── team.js
│   ├── features.js
│   ├── pricingTiers.js
│   ├── pricingSections.js
│   ├── pricingFaq.js
│   ├── faq.js
│   ├── jobs.js
│   ├── partners.js
│   ├── blogPostsData.js
│   ├── companyStats.js
│   ├── companyInformation.js
│   └── *...other data files
├── assets/                      # Images and static assets
│   ├── images/
│   └── socials/
└── stories/                     # Storybook components
    ├── Button.jsx & Button.stories.js
    ├── Header.jsx & Header.stories.js
    ├── Page.jsx & Page.stories.js
    ├── button.css
    ├── header.css
    ├── page.css
    └── assets/

/.storybook
├── main.ts                      # Storybook config
├── preview.tsx                  # Preview settings
└── eval-support/               # Evaluation support files
    ├── transcript.mdx
    ├── summary.mdx
    └── transcript.tsx
```

---

### 12. FORM & VALIDATION PATTERNS

**Forms Present:**
1. **SignUpForm** - Full name, email, password confirmation, reCAPTCHA
2. **SignInForm** - Email, password, reCAPTCHA
3. **ContactForm** - First/Last name, email, company, phone, message, reCAPTCHA
4. **NewsletterForm** - Email only
5. **JobsForm** - Job application form
6. **Profile Edit Forms** - Inline editing for fullname, email, gender, plan, password

**Validation Pattern:**
- Client-side validation with regex (email patterns)
- Errors stored in state object
- ReCAPTCHA required on signup/signin/contact
- reCAPTCHA verification via `VITE_SERVER_URL` endpoint
- Loading states during submission

---

### 13. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES REQUIRED

```
VITE_AIRTABLE_KEY           # Airtable API key
VITE_AIRTABLE_ID            # Airtable base ID
VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL    # Server endpoint for fetching users
VITE_CAPTCHA_KEY            # Google reCAPTCHA site key
VITE_CAPTCHA_SECRET         # Google reCAPTCHA secret
VITE_SERVER_URL             # Backend server URL for captcha verification
```

Backend (`api/.env.backend`):
```
AIRTABLE_KEY
AIRTABLE_ID
CAPTCHA_SECRET
PORT                        # Default 3000
```

---

### 14. KEY HOOKS & DATA FLOWS

**App.jsx Hooks:**
- `useState(user, loggedIn, triggeredLogout)` - auth state
- `useEffect()` - sync sessionStorage with component state on mount/update
- Custom async functions: `retrieveDatabase()`, `registerUser()`, `updateUser()`, `deleteUser()`

**Component Hooks:**
- `useState()` - form state, modal visibility, loading states
- `useRef()` - reCAPTCHA ref for token access
- `useNavigate()` - route navigation on auth success/failure
- `useParams()` - blog post URL params in BlogPost.jsx

**Data Flow:**
```
sessionStorage → App.jsx state → props to Route components → 
Form submission → API calls (Airtable/Server) → Update state → 
sessionStorage → UI re-render
```

---

## SUMMARY

This is a **React 18 SPA with Next.js-like routing** for a social media growth agency ("Edgy"). Key characteristics:

- **Architecture:** SPA with client-side routing (React Router v7), no SSR
- **State Management:** App-level useState, sessionStorage for persistence
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS with custom CSS variables
- **Backend:** Express.js server for reCAPTCHA verification and Airtable updates
- **Database:** Airtable for user data (email, password, profile info)
- **Auth:** Simple email/password with sessionStorage, no JWT/tokens
- **Forms:** Multiple forms with validation and reCAPTCHA protection
- **Modals:** CSS-based fixed overlays (no portals)
- **Testing:** Storybook setup with Vitest and Playwright

All components use **Tailwind utility classes** exclusively (no CSS modules or styled-components).

1→
     2→const blogPostsData = [
     3→    {
     4→        title: "How to Start an Instagram Blog",
     5→        href: "/blog/how-to-start-an-instagram-blog",
     6→        description: "Starting an Instagram blog is relatively easy and can be done in a few simple steps and some creativity as well",
     7→        date: 'Mar 16, 2023',
     8→        datetime: "2023-03-16",
     9→        imageUrl: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600096194534-95cf5ece04cf?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=776&q=80",
    10→        readingTime: '5 min',
    11→        author: {
    12→            name: 'Roel Aufderehar',
    13→            imageUrl: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1472099645785-5658abf4ff4e?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    14→        },
    15→        blogPost: `In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success.\n\n1. Define Your Niche:\nBefore diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content.\n\n2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle:\nYour Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily.\n\n3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic:\nInstagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality.\n\n4. Craft an Engaging Bio:\nYour Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you.\n\n5. Share High-Quality Content:\nQuality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction.\n\n6. Plan Your Content Strategy:\nConsistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche.\n\n7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV:\nInstagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews.\n\n8. Engage with Your Audience:\nInteract with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run.\n\n9. Collaborate and Network:\nCollaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values.\n\n10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt:\nKeep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth.\n\nStarting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!`
    16→
    17→    },
    18→    {
    19→        title: 'What to Post on Instagram',
    20→        href: '/blog/what-to-post-on-instagram',
    21→        description:
    22→            'There are many different types of content that you can post on Instagram to engage with your followers and grow your following.',
    23→        date: 'Mar 10, 2023',
    24→        datetime: '2023-03-10',
    25→        imageUrl:
    26→            'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1611162616305-c69b3fa7fbe0?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=1674&q=80',
    27→        readingTime: '4 min',
    28→        author: {
    29→            name: 'Brenna Goyette',
    30→            imageUrl:
    31→                'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1550525811-e5869dd03032?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    32→        },
    33→        blogPost: `
    34→Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine!\n\n
    35→1. Behind-the-Scenes:\n
    36→Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors.\n\n
    37→2. User-Generated Content (UGC):\n
    38→Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers.\n\n
    39→3. Inspirational Quotes:\n
    40→Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level.\n\n
    41→4. Product or Service Highlights:\n
    42→Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase.\n\n
    43→5. Tutorials and How-Tos:\n
    44→Educate your followers with step-by-step tutorials and how-to guides related to your industry. Providing value through educational content can establish your brand as an authority in your niche.\n\n
    45→6. Customer Testimonials:\n
    46→Share positive feedback and testimonials from satisfied customers. Testimonials build trust and social proof, which can encourage others to do business with you.\n\n
    47→7. Polls and Surveys:\n
    48→Engage your audience by running polls and surveys in your Instagram Stories. This not only encourages interaction but also helps you gather valuable insights from your followers.\n\n
    49→8. Throwback Posts:\n
    50→Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience.\n\n
    51→9. Seasonal Content:\n
    52→Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant.\n\n
    53→10. Fun Facts and Trivia:\n
    54→Share interesting and fun facts related to your industry or niche. Fun and quirky content can entertain your audience and keep them coming back for more.\n\n
    55→11. Collaborations and Partnerships:\n
    56→Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach.\n\n
    57→12. Company Culture:\n
    58→Give your followers a glimpse into your company culture and values. Share team photos, company events, and charitable initiatives.\n\n
    59→Remember, the key to a successful Instagram feed is to mix up your content and keep it engaging. Experiment with different types of posts and see what resonates best with your audience. Happy posting!`
    60→    },
    61→    {
    62→        title: 'Everything You Need To Know About Instagram Creator Studio',
    63→        href: '/blog/wverything-you-need-to-know-about-instagram-creator-studio',
    64→
    65→        description:
    66→            'Instagram Creator Studio is a tool that allows creators and businesses to manage their Instagram content and account from a desktop computer.',
    67→        date: 'Feb 12, 2023',
    68→        datetime: '2023-02-12',
    69→        imageUrl:
    70→            'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1505322747495-6afdd3b70760?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=1770&q=80',
    71→        readingTime: '11 min',
    72→        author: {
    73→            name: 'Daniela Metz',
    74→            imageUrl:
    75→                'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1487412720507-e7ab37603c6f?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    76→        },
    77→        blogPost: `Instagram Creator Studio is a powerful tool that allows creators and businesses to manage their Instagram content and account from a desktop computer. If you want to optimize your Instagram presence and streamline your content creation process, here's everything you need to know about Instagram Creator Studio:\n\n
    78→1. Overview of Features:\n
    79→Instagram Creator Studio offers a range of features to help you manage and analyze your content. You can schedule and publish posts, view post insights, manage comments and direct messages, and track audience demographics.\n\n
    80→2. Content Scheduling:\n
    81→One of the key benefits of Instagram Creator Studio is the ability to schedule your posts in advance. This feature allows you to plan and organize your content calendar, ensuring a consistent posting schedule.\n\n
    82→3. Insights and Analytics:\n
    83→Understanding your audience and post performance is crucial for Instagram success. Instagram Creator Studio provides detailed insights and analytics, allowing you to track the reach, engagement, and impressions of your posts.\n\n
    84→4. Post Management:\n
    85→Easily manage your published posts through Instagram Creator Studio. You can edit captions, add location tags, and even delete or archive posts directly from your desktop.\n\n
    86→5. Content Library:\n
    87→The content library feature enables you to organize your media, making it easier to access and reuse your photos and videos for future posts.\n\n
    88→6. Direct Messaging:\n
    89→Stay connected with your followers by managing your Instagram direct messages through Creator Studio. Respond to messages and engage with your audience efficiently.\n\n
    90→7. Monetization Features:\n
    91→For eligible creators, Instagram Creator Studio provides access to monetization features such as IGTV ads and brand partnerships. This opens up opportunities for generating revenue through your Instagram content.\n\n
    92→8. Video Publishing:\n
    93→Creator Studio also supports video publishing, making it convenient to upload, schedule, and manage your IGTV videos directly from your desktop.\n\n
    94→9. Cross-Platform Management:\n
    95→In addition to Instagram, Creator Studio also allows you to manage content for your Facebook Pages. This integrated platform simplifies content management across both social media platforms.\n\n
    96→10. Accessible for Instagram Business and Creator Accounts:\n
    97→Instagram Creator Studio is available for both business accounts and creator accounts. If you have an Instagram business or creator account, you can access Creator Studio for free.\n\n
    98→In conclusion, Instagram Creator Studio is a valuable tool for Instagram content creators and businesses alike. Its range of features and user-friendly interface make it a must-have for anyone looking to enhance their Instagram presence. Take advantage of Instagram Creator Studio's capabilities and elevate your Instagram strategy to new heights!`
    99→
   100→    },
   101→    {
   102→        title: '20 Tactful Instagram Tips',
   103→        href: '/blog/20-tactful-instagram-tips',
   104→
   105→        description:
   106→            'Here is a collection of 20 tactful Instagram tips that will helps you to grow engagement, increase authentic followers, create quality content and so on',
   107→        date: 'Mar 16, 2023',
   108→        datetime: '2023-03-16',
   109→        imageUrl:

   111→        readingTime: '5 min',
   112→        author: {

   114→            imageUrl:
   115→                'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1487412720507-e7ab37603c6f?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
   116→        },
   117→        blogPost: `Looking to up your Instagram game and boost your engagement? Here are 20 tactful Instagram tips to help you create a compelling and successful presence on the platform:\n\n
   118→1. Define Your Audience:\n
   119→Understand your target audience and create content that resonates with their interests and preferences.\n\n
   120→2. Showcase Your Personality:\n
   121→Let your personality shine through your posts and stories to connect with your followers on a personal level.\n\n
   122→3. Use High-Quality Images:\n
   123→Invest in high-quality photography or use professional editing apps to enhance the visual appeal of your posts.\n\n
   124→4. Craft Engaging Captions:\n
   125→Write captivating captions that complement your images and encourage your followers to interact with your content.\n\n
   126→5. Leverage Hashtags:\n
   127→Use relevant and trending hashtags to expand your reach and attract new followers.\n\n
   128→6. Engage with Your Audience:\n
   129→Respond to comments, direct messages, and mentions to foster a sense of community and build meaningful relationships.\n\n
   130→7. Collaborate with Others:\n
   131→Team up with other creators or brands for collaborations and cross-promotion to reach new audiences.\n\n
   132→8. Utilize Instagram Stories:\n
   133→Take advantage of Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and daily updates.\n\n
   134→9. Post Consistently:\n
   135→Maintain a consistent posting schedule to keep your audience engaged and coming back for more.\n\n
   136→10. Analyze Your Insights:\n
   137→Regularly check your Instagram Insights to understand what content performs best and tailor your strategy accordingly.\n\n
   138→11. Showcase User-Generated Content (UGC):\n
   139→Feature UGC from your followers to foster a sense of community and encourage others to create content related to your brand.\n\n
   140→12. Host Giveaways and Contests:\n
   141→Organize fun giveaways and contests to incentivize engagement and attract new followers.\n\n
   142→13. Share Behind-the-Scenes:\n
   143→Offer a glimpse into your creative process or daily life to make your content more relatable and authentic.\n\n
   144→14. Use Instagram Reels and IGTV:\n
   145→Explore Instagram Reels and IGTV for creating entertaining and longer-form content to diversify your feed.\n\n
   146→15. Optimize Your Bio:\n
   147→Craft a concise and impactful bio that clearly defines your brand and what you offer to your followers.\n\n
   148→16. Monitor Your Competitors:\n
   149→Keep an eye on your competitors' strategies to stay informed about industry trends and stay ahead.\n\n
   150→17. Follow Instagram Trends:\n
   151→Stay updated on the latest Instagram features and trends to keep your content fresh and relevant.\n\n
   152→18. Use Instagram Shopping:\n
   153→Leverage Instagram Shopping to tag products in your posts and stories for a seamless shopping experience.\n\n
   154→19. Track Link Clicks:\n
   155→Use link tracking tools to monitor the performance of links in your bio or swipe-up links in Stories.\n\n
   156→20. Stay Authentic and Transparent:\n
   157→Be true to yourself and your brand values. Authenticity builds trust and loyalty among your followers.\n\n
   158→By implementing these tactful Instagram tips, you can elevate your Instagram presence, foster meaningful connections, and create a vibrant and engaged community of followers. Happy Instagramming!`

   221→        title: 'How to run an Instagram Contest?',
   222→        href: '/blog/how-to-run-an-instagram-contest',
   223→        description:
   224→            'An Instagram contest is a promotional activity where users are asked to submit content (e.g. a photo, video, or text) or perform an action (e.g. like, comment, or share) in exchange for a chance to win a prize',
   225→        date: 'Mar 10, 2023',
   226→        datetime: '2023-03-10',
   227→        imageUrl:
   228→            'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562147458-0c12e8d29f50?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=1770&q=80',
   229→        readingTime: '4 min',
   230→        author: {
   231→            name: 'Roel Aufderehar',
   232→            imageUrl:
   233→                'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1472099645785-5658abf4ff4e?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
   234→        }, blogPost: `Running an Instagram contest can be an exciting way to engage your audience and boost brand awareness. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to run a successful Instagram contest:\n\n
   235→        1. Define Your Goals:\n
   236→        Determine the purpose of your contest. Are you looking to increase followers, generate user-generated content (UGC), or promote a new product?\n\n
   237→        2. Choose the Contest Type:\n
   238→        Decide on the type of contest that aligns with your goals. Popular options include like-to-win, photo contests, and hashtag challenges.\n\n
   239→        3. Set Clear Rules and Guidelines:\n
   240→        Establish straightforward and easy-to-understand rules for participation, eligibility, and prize distribution.\n\n
   241→        4. Select a Relevant Prize:\n
   242→        Offer a prize that resonates with your target audience and encourages them to participate.\n\n
   243→        5. Create Eye-Catching Graphics:\n
   244→        Design visually appealing graphics and promotional materials to announce the contest.\n\n
   245→        6. Decide on the Contest Duration:\n
   246→        Choose a specific start and end date for the contest to create a sense of urgency.\n\n
   247→        7. Promote the Contest:\n
   248→        Use your social media channels, email list, and website to promote the contest and reach a wider audience.\n\n
   249→        8. Monitor Entries and Engage:\n
   250→        Keep track of contest entries, interact with participants, and respond to questions or concerns.\n\n
   251→        9. Choose a Fair Winner:\n
   252→        Select the contest winner fairly and transparently based on the stated rules.\n\n
   253→        10. Announce the Winner:\n
   254→        Publicly announce the winner and thank all participants for their involvement.\n\n
   255→        11. Follow Legal and Instagram Guidelines:\n
   256→        Ensure your contest complies with all relevant laws and Instagram's promotion guidelines.\n\n
   257→        12. Measure Results:\n
   258→        Analyze the success of the contest based on your initial goals and use the insights to improve future contests.\n\n
   259→        13. Repurpose UGC:\n
   260→        Leverage the user-generated content from the contest to showcase your brand and products.\n\n
   261→        14. Express Gratitude:\n
   262→        Show appreciation to your audience for participating and supporting your brand.\n\n
   263→        Running an Instagram contest can not only drive engagement but also foster a sense of community around your brand. By following these steps, you can create a fun and rewarding experience for your followers and achieve your marketing objectives.`

   339→        },
   340→        blogPost: `Promoting your eCommerce store on Instagram can significantly boost your online presence and drive sales. If you're looking to leverage Instagram to grow your eCommerce business, here are some effective strategies to get started:\n\n
   341→1. Optimize Your Instagram Profile:\n
   342→Ensure your Instagram bio clearly communicates your brand, products, and value proposition. Use a recognizable profile picture and include a link to your eCommerce store.\n\n
   343→2. Create High-Quality Visuals:\n
   344→Post eye-catching product photos and lifestyle images that showcase your products in the best light. Use professional photography or high-quality graphics to make your content stand out.\n\n
   345→3. Use Product Tags and Shoppable Posts:\n
   346→Take advantage of Instagram's product tags and shoppable posts features to make it easy for users to shop directly from your posts.\n\n
   347→4. Run Instagram Ads:\n
   348→Invest in Instagram ads to reach a wider audience and promote your best-selling products or special offers.\n\n
   349→5. Host Giveaways and Contests:\n
   350→Organize giveaways and contests to create buzz around your eCommerce store and attract new followers and potential customers.\n\n
   351→6. Collaborate with Influencers:\n
   352→Partner with influencers in your niche to showcase your products to their engaged audience and gain credibility.\n\n
   353→7. Utilize Instagram Stories and Highlights:\n
   354→Use Instagram Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, product demonstrations, and limited-time offers. Save important Stories as Highlights on your profile for easy access.\n\n
   355→8. Offer Exclusive Promotions:\n
   356→Provide exclusive discounts or promotions to your Instagram followers to incentivize them to make a purchase.\n\n
   357→9. Engage with Your Audience:\n
   358→Respond to comments, direct messages, and engage with your followers to build a loyal customer base.\n\n
   359→10. Collaborate with Other Brands:\n
   360→Partner with complementary brands for co-marketing initiatives or joint promotions to expand your reach.\n\n
   361→11. Showcase User-Generated Content (UGC):\n
   362→Repost and feature user-generated content that showcases customers using and enjoying your products.\n\n
   363→12. Leverage Instagram Guides and Reels:\n
   364→Create helpful product guides or entertaining Reels that highlight your products and brand personality.\n\n
   365→13. Utilize Instagram Shopping Features:\n
   366→Make use of Instagram Shopping features like the Shop tab on your profile and the Shopping Explore page to increase product visibility.\n\n
   367→14. Promote Seasonal and Holiday Offers:\n
   368→Plan and promote seasonal sales and holiday offers to capitalize on festive shopping trends.\n\n
   369→15. Monitor and Analyze Performance:\n
   370→Regularly review Instagram Insights and track the performance of your posts and campaigns to refine your strategies.\n\n
   371→16. Showcase Customer Reviews and Testimonials:\n

   374→Host live sessions to showcase new products, conduct Q&A sessions, or share valuable tips with your audience.\n\n

   387→        description:
   388→            'Instagram for business is a way for companies to promote their products or services on the popular social media platform.',
   389→        date: 'Mar 16, 2023',
   390→        datetime: '2023-03-16',
   391→        imageUrl:
   392→            'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1598128558393-70ff21433be0?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=1089&q=80',
   393→        readingTime: '5 min',
   394→        author: {
   395→            name: 'Daniela Metz',
   396→            imageUrl:
   397→                'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1487412720507-e7ab37603c6f?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
   398→        },
   399→        blogPost: `If you're a business looking to harness the power of Instagram for growth and success, this beginner's guide is for you. Instagram is a valuable platform for businesses to connect with their target audience, build brand awareness, and drive sales. Here's how to get started with Instagram for business:\n\n
   400→1. Set Up an Instagram Business Account:\n
   401→Create a dedicated business account on Instagram. It provides access to valuable insights and features like Instagram Ads and Shopping.\n\n
   402→2. Optimize Your Profile:\n
   403→Use your business name as your username and include a recognizable profile picture, preferably your logo. Write a compelling bio that clearly communicates your brand identity and value proposition.\n\n
   404→3. Define Your Target Audience:\n
   405→Identify your target audience and understand their preferences, interests, and pain points. This knowledge will guide your content strategy.\n\n
   406→4. Create High-Quality Visual Content:\n
   407→Invest in creating visually appealing and high-quality images and videos that represent your brand. Use professional photography and graphic design to make your content stand out.\n\n
   408→5. Develop a Content Strategy:\n
   409→Plan your content in advance to ensure consistency and relevance. Mix promotional content with valuable and entertaining posts to keep your audience engaged.\n\n
   410→6. Use Hashtags Strategically:\n
   411→Research and use relevant hashtags to reach a wider audience and increase discoverability. Create a branded hashtag to encourage user-generated content (UGC).\n\n
   412→7. Engage with Your Audience:\n
   413→Respond to comments, messages, and mentions promptly. Engage with your audience through likes, comments, and reposts of UGC.\n\n
   414→8. Utilize Instagram Stories and Highlights:\n
   415→Leverage Instagram Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, product launches, and limited-time offers. Save important Stories as Highlights on your profile to showcase them permanently.\n\n
   416→9. Collaborate with Influencers and Partners:\n
   417→Partner with influencers and complementary businesses to extend your reach and credibility.\n\n
   418→10. Run Instagram Contests and Giveaways:\n
   419→Organize contests and giveaways to boost engagement and attract new followers.\n\n
   420→11. Measure Performance with Instagram Insights:\n
   421→Regularly review Instagram Insights to understand your audience demographics, post reach, and engagement. Use these insights to refine your strategies.\n\n
   422→12. Utilize Instagram Ads:\n
   423→Invest in Instagram Ads to reach a broader audience and promote your products or services effectively.\n\n
   424→13. Monitor Your Competitors:\n
   425→Keep an eye on your competitors' Instagram activities to gain insights into what works and what doesn't in your industry.\n\n
   426→14. Leverage Instagram Shopping Features:\n
   427→Use Instagram Shopping to tag products in your posts and Stories, making it easy for users to shop directly from your content.\n\n
   428→15. Showcase Customer Testimonials:\n
   429→Feature positive customer reviews and testimonials to build trust and credibility.\n\n
   430→16. Analyze and Adjust Your Strategy:\n
   431→Regularly analyze the performance of your Instagram efforts and adjust your strategy based on the results.\n\n
   432→By following these tips and best practices, you'll be well on your way to establishing a strong presence on Instagram and driving business growth. Remember, consistency and authenticity are key to success on this vibrant social platform.`

content.text:
All 13 test files and 22 tests pass (10 new story files + 3 existing example stories).

Here's what was set up:

**Preview setup** (`.storybook/preview.tsx`):
- Imports `../src/core-ui/index.css` (global Tailwind + CSS vars)
- `BrowserRouter` decorator (all components use React Router's `Link`, `NavLink`, `useNavigate`)
- MSW initialized with `onUnhandledRequest: 'bypass'`
- MSW handlers for Airtable API and reCAPTCHA verification

**MSW handlers** (`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`):
- GET/POST handlers for Airtable user database
- POST handler for reCAPTCHA verification

**10 story files** covering the full component spectrum:

| Story file | Type | Stories |
|---|---|---|
| `Navigation.stories.jsx` | Shared component | LoggedOut, LoggedIn |
| `Footer.stories.jsx` | Shared component | Default |
| `Cta.stories.jsx` | Shared component | LoggedOut, LoggedIn |
| `Newsletter.stories.jsx` | Shared component (form) | Default |
| `NotFound.stories.jsx` | Shared component | Default |
| `Landing.stories.jsx` | Full page | Default, LoggedIn |
| `Blog.stories.jsx` | Full page | Default |
| `Pricing.stories.jsx` | Full page | Default, LoggedIn |
| `Faq.stories.jsx` | Full page (HeadlessUI) | Default |
| `Jobs.stories.jsx` | Full page | Default |
